These are all EIS sensors (all have "eis" in their names), so they're all part of the EIS measurement session. Let me now submit with a properly formatted answer.
trace.code:
# Compile results and submit
data = {
    "sensor-eis-freq": 18039.755555555554,
    "sensor-eis-gamma": 319.35,
    "sensor-eis-im": -15.026666666666666,
    "sensor-eis-mag": 38.74777777777778,
    "sensor-eis-phase": -21.94888888888889,
    "sensor-eis-re": 35.791111111111104,
}

answer_text = "Average measured values per sensor for the EIS measurement session:\n"
for sensor, avg in sorted(data.items()):
    answer_text += f"  {sensor}: {avg:.4f}\n"

print(answer_text)

sparql_used = """PREFIX sosa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/>
PREFIX sio: <http://semanticscience.org/resource/>
SELECT ?sensor (AVG(?value) AS ?avgValue) (COUNT(?obs) AS ?count) WHERE {
  GRAPH <http://localhost:8080/graph/observations> {
    ?obs a sosa:Observation ;
         sosa:madeBySensor ?sensor ;
         sio:has-attribute ?mv .
    ?mv sio:has-value ?value .
  }
}
GROUP BY ?sensor
ORDER BY ?sensor"""

sources = [
    "http://localhost:8080/graph/observations",
    "http://localhost:8080/entity/sensor-eis-freq",
    "http://localhost:8080/entity/sensor-eis-gamma",
    "http://localhost:8080/entity/sensor-eis-im",
    "http://localhost:8080/entity/sensor-eis-mag",
    "http://localhost:8080/entity/sensor-eis-phase",
    "http://localhost:8080/entity/sensor-eis-re",
]

SUBMIT(answer_text, sparql_used, sources)
